title: [Bug]: Cannot get podman to switch to networkBackend netavark
body:
### Issue Description

Per this blog post:
https://blog.podman.io/2023/01/podman-begins-cni-plugins-deprecation/

I read that I should be able to switch the networkbackEnd from CNI to netavaark.

I followed those instructions for changing it and after completing the final step:  podman system reset,   CNI was still the network backend.

podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Jan 12 05:31:26 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64


 rpm -q podman
podman-4.2.0-6.module+el8.7.0+1122+04a7498b.x86_64

Also I have verified netavark and aardvark-dns

 rpm -q netavark
netavark-1.1.0-7.module+el8.7.0+1122+04a7498b.x86_64

 rpm -q aardvark-dns
aardvark-dns-1.1.0-5.module+el8.7.0+1122+04a7498b.x86_64




### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  Verified my podman version was at least version 4, and ensured the netavark and aarkvark-dns were installed.
2. ran:  podman system reset
3. 


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received
The networkBackend is still CNI.

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected
I expected the networkBackend to change to netavark.

comments.body:
RHEL 8 and thus I assume rocky as well still uses CNI, this is forced via containers.conf option. 
If you want to change this you need to change the `network_backend` option in containers.conf yourself.
